bands: 364.2 - 365.5 x sqrt(1 - ((waist/(2·pi))/(0.5·height))^2). Higher = greater central

Low risk when all five are negative; ~96% sensitivity as a rule-out. A risk category, not

bands: A ratio ≤ 38 rules preeclampsia out for the next week (NPV ≈ 99.3%, Roche Elecsys assay). A

sfsr.name: San Francisco Syncope Rule (CHESS)
sfsr.summary: San Francisco Syncope Rule (CHESS): high risk for a serious 7-day outcome if any of congestive heart failure history, hematocrit < 30%, abnormal ECG, shortness of breath, or triage SBP < 90.
bands: Any factor present: higher risk of a serious 30-day outcome.
No factor present: lower risk of a serious 30-day outcome.

sga-nutrition.name: Subjective Global Assessment (SGA, Detsky)
sga-nutrition.summary: Subjective Global Assessment (Detsky 1987): the reference bedside malnutrition assessment, a structured clinician gestalt over history and exam features yielding an overall A (well nourished), B
bands: Five history features (weight change, intake change, GI symptoms > 2 weeks, functional
A (well nourished), B (moderately or suspected malnourished), or C (severely malnourished).

sgarbossa.name: Modified Sgarbossa Criteria (Smith)
sgarbossa.summary: Modified Sgarbossa criteria for acute MI in left bundle branch block or ventricular pacing: positive if any of concordant ST elevation >= 1 mm, concordant ST depression >= 1 mm in V1-V3, or an ST/S
bands: Highly specific for acute myocardial infarction in the setting of LBBB or paced rhythm.
Does not rule out MI; the modified Sgarbossa criteria may add sensitivity per source.

shaffer-angle.name: Shaffer Gonioscopy Angle Grade
shaffer-angle.summary: Shaffer gonioscopy grading (Shaffer 1960) of the anterior chamber (drainage) angle (grades 0-4) - used to gauge the risk of angle-closure glaucoma. A HIGHER grade is a WIDER, safer angle. 4: wide
bands: Open angle, incapable of closure. 4: wide open (~35-45 deg). 3: open (~20-35 deg).
Moderately narrow (~20 deg); angle closure is possible.
1: very narrow (~10 deg), closure probable. 0: closed. High angle-closure risk.

shine-lal-index.name: Shine & Lal Index
shine-lal-index.summary: Shine & Lal index (1977) = (MCV^2 x MCH) / 100; < 1530 suggests beta-thalassemia trait, > 1530 iron deficiency.
bands: Beta-thalassemia trait suggested.
Iron-deficiency suggested ((MCV^2 x MCH) / 100; cutoff varies by population). A

shock-index.name: MAP / Pulse Pressure / Shock Index
shock-index.summary: Perfusion panel: shock index (HR/SBP, concerning >= 0.9), modified shock index (HR/MAP), MAP, and pulse pressure from SBP/DBP/HR.

shorr.name: Shorr Score (MRSA Pneumonia Risk)
shorr.summary: Shorr MRSA-pneumonia risk score - a weighted count predicting MRSA in culture-positive pneumonia (a total marking > 30% MRSA prevalence is high risk). Factors: recent hospitalization (+2), ICU
bands: Low - MRSA < 10% per source; anti-MRSA therapy may be safely withheld.
Medium MRSA risk.
High - MRSA > 30% per source. It reports MRSA risk, not a vancomycin/linezolid order.

short-physical-performance-battery.name: Short Physical Performance Battery (SPPB)

Normal ~4-10%; rising with true shunt.

bands: No raphe; two (usually symmetrical) leaflets and two commissures.
One raphe (the most common); sub-typed by the fused sinuses (L-R, R-N, or N-L).
